perturbation.original_text: HSNS shall maintain complete and accurate records of its activities under this Agreement for at least two (2) years following termination of this Agreement. HSNS agrees to allow E.piphany, directly or indirectly, to audit HSNS's business records as kept by HSNS in its normal course of business to ensure compliance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. If the audit reveals that HSNS has failed to comply with the terms of this Agreement, in addition to all other remedies available to E.piphany at law or equity, HSNS shall immediately reimburse E.piphany for any unpaid amounts due and the cost of the audit.
perturbation.changed_text: HSNS should endeavor to maintain records of its activities under this Agreement, if practicable. HSNS will consider allowing E.piphany, directly or indirectly, to conduct a review of HSNS's business records. If a review suggests HSNS may not have fully complied with the terms of this Agreement, HSNS may consider appropriate remedial actions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'shall maintain complete and accurate records' with 'should endeavor to maintain records, if practicable,' making record-keeping discretionary. The phrase 'will consider allowing' weakens the obligation to allow audits, and 'may consider appropriate remedial actions' creates uncertainty about consequences of non-compliance. This overall weakens the legal obligation to comply with the terms of the contract and fails to create an obligation for HSNS to cooperate with E.piphany.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Failure to maintain accurate records and provide access during audits contradicts generally accepted accounting principles and contract law principles regarding good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 1304 states that 'Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' By weakening the obligation to maintain records and allow audits, the modified text allows HSNS to avoid accountability. This undermines E.piphany's ability to ensure compliance with the contract terms and collect appropriate payments, violating the principle of good faith and fair dealing required under California law.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 3. Territorial Applicability and General Rules [1301 - 1310] ( Chapter 3 added by Stats. 2006, Ch. 254, Sec. 30. ) 1304. Every contract or duty within this code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. (Added by renumbering Section 1203 by Stats. 2006, Ch. 254, Sec. 19. Effective January 1, 2007.)
perturbation.justification: The modified contract language weakens the obligation to maintain accurate records and allow audits, potentially violating the duty of good faith as stated in California Commercial Code § 1304. The original text mandated maintaining records and allowing audits ('shall maintain,' 'agrees to allow'), while the changed text makes these actions discretionary ('should endeavor, if practicable,' 'will consider allowing'). This shift undermines E.piphany's ability to ensure compliance and collect payments, contradicting the 'obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement' as described in Scraped Law Snippet 1.

perturbation.original_text: HSNS agrees that, at all times and in every respect during the term of this Agreement, it shall conduct its business in a professional manner consistent with E.piphany's norms and standards, which shall reflect favorably upon E.piphany and the Application.
perturbation.changed_text: HSNS will aim to conduct its business in a manner that it reasonably believes is generally consistent with professional standards, taking into account its own business practices.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires HSNS to conduct business in a professional manner consistent with E.piphany's norms and standards. The modified version introduces ambiguity by stating HSNS will 'aim' to conduct business and 'reasonably believes' it is consistent with professional standards. It also allows HSNS to consider its own business practices which may deviate from E.piphany's. This weakening makes it difficult to enforce a specific standard of conduct and contradicts general business practices.
perturbation.contradicted_law: This modification introduces ambiguity concerning the standard of professional conduct, contradicting principles of good faith and fair dealing in contractual obligations, as well as potential implications under business ethics regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code § 17200 prohibits unfair competition, including unfair or fraudulent business acts or practices. By allowing HSNS to subjectively determine what is 'generally consistent with professional standards' and prioritizing 'its own business practices', the change weakens HSNS's commitment to act fairly. This can lead to HSNS engaging in practices that, while deemed acceptable by them, may unfairly harm E.piphany's reputation or business interests and constitute unfair competition.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 17200 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 5. Enforcement [17200 - 17210] ( Chapter 5 added by Stats. 1977, Ch. 299. ) 17200. As used in this chapter, unfair competition shall mean and include any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business act or practice and unfair, deceptive, untrue or misleading advertising and any act prohibited by Chapter 1 (commencing with Section 17500) of Part 3 of Division 7 of the Business and Professions Code. (Amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 430, Sec. 2. Effective January 1, 1993.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity and weakens the original obligation, creating a contradiction with California Business and Professions Code § 17200. The original text requires HSNS to conduct business in a professional manner consistent with E.piphany's norms and standards. The modified version allows HSNS to 'aim' to conduct business and 'reasonably believes' it is consistent with professional standards, also prioritizing its own business practices, which may deviate from E.piphany's. According to Scraped Law Snippet 1, BPC 17200 defines unfair competition as 'any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business act or practice'. The change weakens HSNS's commitment to act fairly, potentially harming E.piphany's business interests.
